migrations.description: Rename CkArchive (concrete raw-archive default) to CkRawArchive. CkArchive becomes the abstract base for raw / rollup / time-range subtypes.

scripts.description:
Migration from System.StreamData 1.1.0 to 1.2.0.

Type renames (Phase 2 of the time-range concept — see
octo-construction-kit-engine/docs/concept-time-range-archives.md):
- CkArchive (was a concrete raw archive default) is split:
  - Archive becomes the abstract base.
  - RawArchive (new concrete subtype) inherits the today's raw-archive
    semantics. Existing entities are rewritten to RawArchive.
- CkRollupArchive is renamed RollupArchive. No semantic change; the
  type just drops the misleading Ck prefix (Ck was for the CK type
  system itself, not for runtime entity types).

Records (CkArchiveColumn, CkRollupAggregation) and enums
(CkArchiveStatus, CkRollupFunction) keep their Ck-prefixed names in
this migration. Renaming them requires a `ChangeCkRecordId` /
`ChangeCkEnumId` migration action which the engine doesn't yet
support; that cleanup lands in Phase 2.5 once the framework is
extended.

The per-archive CrateDB tables are unaffected: the physical table
name is derived from the entity rtid only, not from the CK type
discriminator.

Breaking changes:
- Callers of the auto-generated `runtime.systemStreamDataCkArchives
  .create` GraphQL mutation must switch to
  `runtime.systemStreamDataRawArchives.create`. The CkRollupArchive
  create mutation is replaced by `systemStreamDataRollupArchives`.
- C# code that instantiates `new RtCkArchive { ... }` or
  `new RtCkRollupArchive { ... }` must switch to `new RtRawArchive`
  / `new RtRollupArchive`. The abstract `Archive` base cannot be
  instantiated.

steps.description: Promote existing CkArchive entities to the new concrete RawArchive subtype.
Strip the Ck prefix from existing CkRollupArchive entities — no semantic change, just a rename.

postValidations.description: Verify no entities remain with the old abstract-base ckTypeId.
Verify no entities remain with the old CkRollupArchive ckTypeId.

types.description: Abstract base for a CrateDB-backed time-series archive. Concrete subtypes: RawArchive (instant measurements with a single timestamp), RollupArchive (system-orchestrated bucket aggregations of another archive), TimeRangeArchive (externally aggregated values with [from, to) windows — added in a later concept). Shared attributes: TargetCkTypeId, Columns, Status, optional RawRetentionMs. Subtypes add their own time-axis semantics.

Concrete Archive subtype for instant time-series measurements — one row per data point with a single timestamp. External callers (MeshAdapter pipelines, REST clients) push StreamDataPoints via SaveStreamDataInArchive@1 / IStreamDataRepository.InsertAsync. No source archive, no orchestrator. The 1.1.0 de-facto raw-archive shape, lifted out of what's now the abstract Archive base.

Concrete Archive subtype for derived bucketed aggregations of another archive (raw or rollup-of-rollup). Columns are generated from Aggregations[] at activation time and immutable afterwards. Inserts happen exclusively through the system-internal rollup orchestrator. The inherited Columns attribute is populated automatically; direct edits are rejected by validation.

Concrete Archive subtype for externally pre-aggregated time-range data. Each row carries an explicit [from, to) window (a System/TimeRange record on the time axis at the storage layer) instead of a single timestamp. External systems (EDA reports, smart meters, weather APIs) push data directly via SaveTimeRangeStreamDataInArchive@1 / IStreamDataRepository.InsertTimeRangeAsync — there is no source archive and no system orchestrator on this path. Concept doc: docs/concept-time-range-archives.md.

attributes.description: Set of columns (attribute paths) the archive captures. At least one column is required.

Retention window for raw data points in milliseconds. When set, the partition pruner may drop source partitions older than (now - RawRetentionMs), but never ahead of the slowest attached rollup's watermark. Null means keep forever.
Lifecycle status of the archive. Defaults to Created.
Construction Kit type ID that this archive captures time-series data for.
When true (default), the generated CrateDB column is indexed by the standard rules; when false, the column is created with INDEX OFF. Disable only for columns that are read but never filtered.
Attribute path captured by this column. Supports nested record traversal (e.g. 'sensor.reading.value') and array projection (e.g. 'readings[*].value').
When true, every insert must supply a non-null value for this path; missing values cause an exception. When false, missing values are stored as NULL and preserved across upserts via COALESCE.
Aggregation function applied to SourcePath within each bucket.
Attribute path on the source archive (must exist in the source's Columns list at activation time). For chained rollups referencing a stored AVG, use the explicit _sum / _count column names.
Optional explicit storage column name. Default is '{sourcePath}_{function}' lower-cased; for AVG, two columns are created with suffixes _sum and _count derived from this base name.
List of aggregation definitions. At least one entry is required at activation time. Immutable after activation; schema changes require creating a new rollup.

Bucket-boundary alignment. FixedSize (default, key 0) preserves the legacy 'LastAggregatedBucketEnd + BucketSizeMs' arithmetic. Calendar/Iso8601 values align bucket boundaries to wall-clock UTC boundaries so monthly / weekly / yearly rollups become expressible without forcing BucketSizeMs into a single TimeSpan. Pre-1.4.0 entities resolve to FixedSize at read time. Immutable after activation.

Width of the aggregation bucket in milliseconds. Each output row covers the half-open interval [bucketEnd - BucketSizeMs, bucketEnd). Must be positive and a multiple of the source rollup's bucket size when chaining. Immutable after activation.

When set, the rollup is read-only up to this timestamp: the orchestrator will not produce new buckets whose bucketEnd is ≤ FrozenUntil. Set automatically when the source archive is truncated for the covered range, so the already-aggregated history is preserved. Monotonic; can only move forward.

Exclusive end timestamp of the most recently committed bucket. Null before the first run. Maintained exclusively by the rollup orchestrator; manual edits go through the rewindRollupWatermark mutation.
Runtime id of the source Archive this rollup aggregates from. May reference another RollupArchive to form a chain (e.g. raw → 1 min → 1 h). Immutable after the rollup is activated.
Lower bound on how far behind real-time the orchestrator stays before closing a bucket, in milliseconds. Absorbs late-arriving source inserts. Default 300000 (five minutes). Mutable.

Advisory window length for the rows this archive holds (e.g. PT15M, PT1H, P1D). Optional and descriptive only — the engine does not enforce that incoming [from, to) windows match the declared period. Used by the studio to label the archive ('EDA 15-min') and by chained-rollup recommendations. Variable-period archives are allowed (leave null).

records.description: Single column definition inside a CkArchive: the attribute path to capture and whether it is required and indexed.

Single aggregation definition for a CkRollupArchive. Maps a source archive column (SourcePath) to one or more storage columns via Function. AVG materialises as two stored columns (sum + count); the other functions map 1:1.

values.name: FixedSize
values.description: Default. Each bucket spans exactly BucketSizeMs; boundaries are LastAggregatedBucketEnd, LastAggregatedBucketEnd + BucketSizeMs, ...

values.name: CalendarDay
values.description: Bucket boundaries align to UTC calendar days (00:00:00 UTC). BucketSizeMs is informational only.

values.name: Iso8601Week
values.description: Bucket boundaries align to ISO-8601 weeks (Monday 00:00:00 UTC to next Monday 00:00:00 UTC). BucketSizeMs is informational only.

values.name: CalendarMonth
values.description: Bucket boundaries align to UTC calendar months (first day of month 00:00:00 UTC). BucketSizeMs is informational only; month lengths vary 28-31 days.

values.name: CalendarYear
values.description: Bucket boundaries align to UTC calendar years (Jan 1 00:00:00 UTC). BucketSizeMs is informational only.

enums.description: Bucket-boundary alignment for a RollupArchive. FixedSize (default) uses the legacy LastAggregatedBucketEnd + BucketSizeMs arithmetic. The other values align to calendar boundaries — BucketSizeMs is interpreted as a lower bound (e.g. 28d for monthly) but the actual boundary comes from the calendar so monthly/weekly EDA rollups become expressible. Concept-time-range §7.

values.name: Created
values.description: Archive definition exists, but no Crate table has been provisioned. Inserts and queries are rejected.

values.name: Activated
values.description: Crate table exists, schema is frozen, inserts and queries are accepted.

values.name: Disabled
values.description: Crate table exists, but inserts and queries are rejected. Data is preserved.

values.name: Failed
values.description: Activation failed; manual retry required. Inserts and queries are rejected.

enums.description: Lifecycle status of a CkArchive instance.

values.name: Avg
values.description: Arithmetic mean. Stored as sum and count columns; the average is computed on read.

values.name: Sum
values.description: Sum of values in the bucket.

values.name: Count
values.description: Number of non-null values in the bucket.

enums.description: Aggregation function used by a CkRollupAggregation. AVG is stored as two columns (sum + count) so chained rollups stay numerically correct.

description: StreamData model. Defines the abstract Archive base, RawArchive (instant measurements), RollupArchive (derived bucketed-aggregation archives), TimeRangeArchive (externally pre-aggregated [from, to) windowed data), and supporting enum/record types. 1.1.0 → 1.2.0 renamed CkArchive → Archive (abstract) + introduced RawArchive; 1.2.0 → 1.3.0 adds TimeRangeArchive (additive, no entity migration needed); 1.3.0 → 1.4.0 adds RollupArchive.BucketAlignment + the BucketAlignment enum so monthly / weekly / yearly rollups are expressible (additive, pre-1.4.0 entities resolve to FixedSize). Loaded only when StreamData is enabled at the instance level.
